Commercial Mar 25, 2026

10 Boeing Jets With The Longest Range Flying Today

Boeing’s longest-range jets reach over 8,500 NM, enabling ultra-long-haul flights.

Ultra-long-range Boeing aircraft enable direct point-to-point routes that reduce fuel costs and crew fatigue while minimizing ground handling requirements.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

FAA Clears Higher Takeoff Weights For Boeing 787-9, -10

The FAA has approved higher takeoff weights for the Boeing 787-9 and 787-10, giving operators more flexibility to carry additional payload or extend range on long-haul routes.

Higher certified weights unlock revenue opportunities for 787 operators by enabling payload-range tradeoffs previously constrained by regulatory limits.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Suspends Flights On Major Long-Haul Route (Full Schedule Inside)

Delta's latest development follows other airlines in the market. See the latest information here!

Delta's route suspension reflects industry-wide capacity adjustments as carriers reassess profitability on specific long-haul markets amid demand fluctuations.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Awards Astrophysics Postdoctoral Fellowships for 2026

The highly competitive NASA Hubble Fellowship Program (NHFP) recently named 24 new fellows to its 2026 class. The NHFP enables outstanding postdoctoral scientists to pursue independent research in any area of NASA Astrophysics, using theory, observations, simulations, experimentation, or instrument...

NASA's competitive fellowship program attracts top postdoctoral talent to advance astrophysics research, strengthening the scientific workforce supporting space exploration missions and satellite technology development.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA-ISRO Satellite Captures Pacific Northwest Through Clouds

Seattle and Portland, Oregon, are among the cloudiest cities in the United States. But that infamous cloud cover is no match for the U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar), which is designed to peer straight through clouds. Doing so allows scientists to study the...

NISAR's all-weather synthetic aperture radar capability enables continuous Earth monitoring over persistently cloudy regions, reducing data gaps that traditionally limited climate and disaster-response planning for aviation operations.

See NASA →

PAI
Safety Mar 25, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective risk controls require organizations to actively document and justify acceptance of remaining risks rather than simply implementing mitigations.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Military Mar 25, 2026

New Images Show B-2 Spirit Bombers Departing for Iran Mission with Unknown White Patches on Wings

Recently released photos show B-2 stealth bombers preparing to launch for a mission over Iran last week, with multiple unidentified white patches added along the leading edges of their wings. The U.S. Central Command has released new photos of the B-2A Spirit stealth bomber, one of the assets that,...

B-2 Spirit bombers deployed to Iran featured unexplained white patches on wing leading edges, suggesting possible operational modifications or countermeasure applications not previously disclosed.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

Digital Airman Certificate Bill Clears U.S. House

Measure would permit pilots to present electronic credentials during FAA inspections.

Digitizing airman certificates could accelerate pilot credential verification during audits while reducing administrative friction in general aviation operations.

See AVweb →

Military Mar 25, 2026

Brazil unveils first Gripen E fighter assembled on home soil

Brazil has unveiled the first Gripen E fighter assembled on its own soil, a milestone for the Saab-Embraer…

Brazil's domestic Gripen E assembly capability strengthens Embraer's aerospace manufacturing portfolio and positions the nation as a regional defense contractor, potentially enabling future export opportunities.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations with psychologically safe environments see higher incident reporting rates, enabling proactive hazard identification before accidents occur.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

China Eastern orders over 100 Airbus aircraft, plans to retire at least 53 A320s

China Eastern Airlines has placed a $15.802 billion Airbus order for 101 A320neo family aircraft, comprised of A320neo,…

China Eastern's massive $15.8B order signals a fleet modernization strategy that will reduce older A320 operational costs while expanding capacity, offsetting some aircraft retirements.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Telesat eyes defense role with laser comms test ahead of LEO debut

Canadian operator adds military Ka-band and plans 2027 NASA demonstration as Pentagon seeks faster space data transport

Telesat's laser communication demonstration positions LEO satellite operators as critical infrastructure for military data networks, opening a new revenue stream beyond commercial broadband.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 25, 2026

UK, Turkey sign Eurofighter Typhoon support deal to advance fighter export

The UK and Turkey have signed a new support and training agreement tied to Ankara’s Eurofighter Typhoon purchase,…

Turkey's Eurofighter support deal strengthens UK defense export competitiveness while establishing a template for future allied fighter jet sustainment partnerships.

See AeroTime Hub →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Medium-sized regional airports like CID serve critical dual roles in supporting both flight training pipelines and scheduled commercial service, making them essential infrastructure for workforce development.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Sift raises $42 million in Series B round

WASHINGTON – Sift, a Southern California startup developing tools to help engineers make sense of hardware sensor data, raised $42 million in a Series B investment round. With the funding, Sift plans to expand its staff of engineers building the infrastructure layer that underpins devices...

Sift's $42M Series B validates growing market demand for sensor data analytics infrastructure across hardware industries, positioning data interpretation as a critical competitive advantage for aerospace and defense manufacturers.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Wizz Air CCO on recovering the cost lead, offering Wi-Fi, and hedging fuel

On March 25, 2026, Wizz Air announced that it had signed a deal with Barcelona-based inflight technology provider…

Low-cost carriers are now competing on connectivity and passenger experience, not just airfare, signaling a shift in how budget airlines capture ancillary revenue.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

House Moves to Bring Back Civil Overland Supersonic Flight

Measure would direct FAA to write new regulations for civil aircraft flying faster than Mach 1.

Congressional action to legalize overland supersonic flight could unlock a $100+ billion market opportunity while requiring FAA to solve decades-old sonic boom mitigation challenges.

See AVweb →

▶
Safety Mar 25, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit confusion at LAX's Runway 24R demonstrates how high-traffic airport infrastructure design can create unintended pilot navigation errors despite clear markings.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Make-Or-Break Week: United Airlines Enters Final Contract Talks With Flight Attendants

Representatives of the carrier's cabin crew are hoping to be able to lock down a tentative agreement.

United's flight attendant negotiations entering final stages could set wage and contract benchmarks affecting labor costs across the entire industry.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy signals intensifying competition in the consumer drone market, potentially reshaping purchasing patterns and forcing competitors to recalibrate their market positioning.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air Arabia debuts Panasonic Avionics multi-orbit IFC

Already a customer of Panasonic Avionics’ eXW wireless inflight entertainment system, UAE-based Air Arabia is now flying with the IFEC giant’s Ku-band multi-orbit onboard connectivity solution, Runway Girl Network can reveal. The all-Airbus A320 Family operator is among a cluster of carriers...

Air Arabia's adoption of multi-orbit Ku-band connectivity signals growing carrier preference for hybrid satellite solutions that balance coverage reliability across global routes without single-provider dependency.

See Runway Girl Network →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrating AI-driven traffic management with sustainable fuel adoption can simultaneously increase airport capacity by 15-20% while reducing aviation's carbon footprint.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

FAA Letter Says Whiteman Airport Obligated to Stay Open

Los Angeles County still continues to study closure.

Despite LA County's ongoing closure study, FAA obligations may legally prevent Whiteman Airport from shuttering, creating potential conflict between local land-use goals and federal aviation policy.

See AVweb →

International Mar 25, 2026

Philippine carriers cut flights, secure fuel as government declares emergency

The Philippines has become the first country to declare a national state of energy emergency amid escalating Middle…

Airlines facing prolonged fuel supply disruptions may need to implement capacity reductions rather than temporary schedule adjustments, signaling potential structural challenges to regional aviation networks.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Southwest Airlines Cabin Reconfiguration Increases Seat Pitch, Reducing Overall Passenger Capacity

Southwest Airlines has added extra-legroom seats to its fleet.

Southwest's capacity reduction trade-off signals that premium comfort differentiation may outweigh revenue gains from higher load factors in competitive markets.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Infinity Avionics Unveils Aquila: The Next-Generation Space Imaging Solution for Complex Orbital Operations

CANBERRA, Australia: Infinity Avionics, a leader in smart vision systems for the NewSpace economy, announced today the full commercial availability of Aquila. Following a standout debut at IAC Sydney, the […]

Aquila's commercial release addresses a critical capability gap in autonomous orbital operations by integrating advanced vision processing that reduces ground station dependency for complex satellite maneuvers.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

$15.8 Billion Vote Of Confidence: Airbus Scores Massive 101-Plane Deal

The European planemaker continues to make big steps forward in a key market.

Airbus's 101-plane order signals sustained demand recovery and validates its competitive positioning against Boeing amid ongoing supply chain normalization.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Can’t The Airbus A350 Be Powered By Any Other Engine Type?

The Airbus A350 and Rolls-Royce Trent XWB are a match made in aviation heaven.

The A350's airframe design, systems integration, and weight distribution were engineered exclusively around the Trent XWB's specifications, making engine re-certification technically and economically impractical.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

Airbase raises $5 million to tackle spectrum bottleneck as FCC eyes new space uses

New York-based software startup Airbase emerged from stealth March 25 after raising $5 million to modernize how governments coordinate radio frequencies used by satellites, 5G networks and other wireless systems.

Airbase's spectrum management software addresses a critical infrastructure gap that could unlock FCC approval for new satellite and wireless applications competing for increasingly congested frequency bands.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Flight Halted After Bizarre Runway Encounter With Coyote

The aircraft did not suffer any damage and was back in the air shortly after the incident, transporting passengers to their original destination.

Wildlife incursions at airports highlight operational vulnerabilities that can ground aircraft even when no damage occurs, underscoring need for enhanced perimeter security.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Warehouse drones are quietly transforming alcohol distribution across America

In a move that shows just how fast warehouse automation is evolving, Corvus Robotics has teamed up with Southern Glazer’s Wine & Spirits to scale autonomous inventory drones across one of the largest beverage distribution networks in the US. more…

Autonomous inventory drones are moving beyond theoretical applications into large-scale commercial logistics, signaling regulatory acceptance and operational viability for warehouse automation in regulated industries.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Musk wants to go to the moon. But how will he build his ‘self-growing city’?

It goes without saying that we shouldn’t take everything Elon Musk says too seriously. There are whole websites dedicated to those times when he has ‘talked the talk’ but failed to ‘walk the walk’ — as well as those times when he has been factually untrue. But his latest claim, which is that he...

Musk's lunar city vision hinges on SpaceX's ability to develop rapid reusable rocket technology and in-situ resource utilization at scale, both requiring breakthroughs beyond current aerospace capabilities.

See SpaceNews →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Bay Area next as Wing expands drone delivery network

The Wing team has announced plans to expand its ultra-fast residential drone delivery service across the San Francisco Bay Area in the coming months, marking a full-circle moment for the company that was originally born inside Google X back in 2012. more…

Wing's Bay Area expansion signals that autonomous residential delivery is transitioning from experimental to mainstream infrastructure across major metropolitan markets.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

14+ Hours Nonstop: Delta Air Lines Reveals Brand New Long-Haul Route

Delta Air Lines has disclosed another new long-haul route: Los Angeles to Manila. While it has not been officially announced yet, it was revealed in the airline's response to Philippine Airlines' and its application to the US Department of Transportation to begin flights from Manila to Chicago...

Delta's Manila route announcement signals competitive positioning against Philippine Airlines' US expansion plans, likely driven by growing Southeast Asian demand and bilateral route allocation dynamics.

See Simple Flying →

Air Cargo Mar 25, 2026

Meet The New King Of Cargo: Atlas Air Orders 20 Airbus A350Fs

Well, it looks like in a few years’ time, the United States will have ANOTHER A350-operator alongside Delta… but it won’t be flying passengers! That’s because Airbus announced on March 16th that Atlas Air Worldwide has placed a firm order for 20 Airbus A350F freighters, making it the largest...

Atlas Air's A350F order signals a fundamental shift in cargo aircraft economics, with wide-body freighters now competing directly with narrow-body converted freighters for long-haul freight routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Porter to launch 12 routes out of new Montreal Metropolitan Airport terminal

Porter Airlines will be the launch carrier at Montreal Metropolitan Airport (YHU) brand new terminal. Starting on June…

Porter's expansion into Montreal's new regional hub positions the carrier to capture leisure and business travel from a secondary airport with lower congestion costs than established hubs.
